pi_items.source: Multiple chemical databases (ChemicalBook, Engineering Toolbox); CAMEO Chemicals NOAA database
SG 0.88 < 1.0 and benzene is slightly soluble in water (188 mg/100 mL). Matches classification rule: SG < 1.0 + Insoluble/Slightly Soluble = FLOATS
CAMEO Chemicals (cameochemicals.noaa.gov) official database; ChemicalBook; CDC NIOSH Pocket Guide
SG 1.59 > 1.0 and CCl4 is slightly soluble in water (0.05%, ~1.2 g/L). Matches classification rule: SG > 1.0 + Insoluble/Slightly Soluble = SINKS
ChemicalBook; Shell Chemical datasheet; Multiple chemical property databases
Acetone is soluble in all proportions (miscible) with water. Matches classification rule: Soluble or Miscible in water = DISSOLVES
CAMEO Chemicals (cameochemicals.noaa.gov) CHRIS document; ChemicalBook; PubChem
SG 1.49 > 1.0 and chloroform is slightly soluble in water (8-10.1 g/L). Matches classification rule: SG > 1.0 + Insoluble/Slightly Soluble = SINKS
CAMEO Chemicals (cameochemicals.noaa.gov); ChemicalBook; PubChem; CDC NIOSH Pocket Guide
SG 0.87 < 1.0 and toluene is slightly soluble in water (0.52 g/L). Matches classification rule: SG < 1.0 + Insoluble/Slightly Soluble = FLOATS
Reasonable tolerance for specific gravity measurements (±0.05 is standard in chemical analysis). All verified SG values fall within this tolerance of metadata values.

pi_items.verified_value: CAMEO Chemicals is a real, official NOAA database maintained by the Office of Response and Restoration, National Ocean Service, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
pi_items.source: Official NOAA website verification; CAMEO Chemicals is publicly accessible with thousands of chemical datasheets including hazard information, first aid, and emergency response data

privileged_info_summary: All metadata values for the five chemicals have been independently verified: Benzene (SG 0.88, FLOATS), Carbon tetrachloride (SG 1.59, SINKS), Acetone (SG 0.79, DISSOLVES), Chloroform (SG 1.49, SINKS), and Toluene (SG 0.87, FLOATS). All specific gravity values match authoritative chemical databases with appropriate rounding. All spill behavior classifications are correct based on the stated rules: chemicals with SG < 1.0 and low water solubility float, those with SG > 1.0 and low solubility sink, and acetone which is miscible with water dissolves. The data source is the real CAMEO Chemicals database maintained by NOAA (cameochemicals.noaa.gov), not synthetic data.
